channel.title: 1 Samuel on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in 1 Samuel on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: 1 Samuel 1
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 1

The Birth of Samuel

1 Elkanah was from Ramathaim-zophim in the hill country of Ephraim. He was the son of Jeroham son of Elihu (son of Tohu son of Zuph), an Ephraimite. 2 He had two wives: Hannah and Peninnah. Peninnah had children but Hannah didn’t.

item.title: 1 Samuel 2
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 2

He worshipped the LORD there.

1 Sam 1:21-28

Hannah’s Prayer

1 Then Hannah prayed,

                        “My heart celebrates in the LORD;

                              you’ve honored me.

                        I speak boldly against my adversaries

                              because I celebrate over your salvation.

                        2 There’s no one holy like the LORD;

item.title: 1 Samuel 3
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 3

The Call of Samuel

1 The boy Samuel continued to serve the LORD with Eli. A message from the LORD rarely came at that time; visions weren’t frequent. 2 Eli was lying down. (His eyesight had gotten bad.) 3 The LORD’s lamp hadn’t gone out yet, and Samuel was lying down in the Tabernacle where the Ark of God was. 4 The LORD called out to him. He said, “Yes?” 5 He ran to Eli and said, “You called.” But he said, “I didn’t call; lie back down.” So he went and lay down. 6 The LORD called out again, “Samuel.” He got up and went to Eli, “You called.” But he answered, “I didn’t call you, son; lie back down.” 7 Samuel didn’t know the LORD yet, and the LORD hadn’t revealed a message to him before. 8 The LORD called out to him the third time. He got up and went to Eli, “You called.” Eli surmised that the LORD was calling the boy. 9 He told him, “Go lie down. If he calls you, say, ‘Yes, LORD; I’m listening.’” So he went and lay down.

item.title: 1 Samuel 4
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 4

The Philistines Capture the Ark

1 Samuel’s word went out to all Israel.

[4:1]Israel had gone out to meet the Philistines in battle and camped beside Ebenezer. The Philistines camped in Aphek 2 and drew up in formation against Israel. When the battle spread, the Philistines defeated Israel and killed about 4,000. 3 When the troops came to camp, Israel’s elders said,

item.title: 1 Samuel 5
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 5

The Philistines and the Ark

1 The Philistines carried the Ark from Ebenezer to Ashdod 2 to Dagon’s temple and set it up beside him. 3 When people got up early the next morning, Dagon had fallen face down on the ground in front of the Ark of the LORD. They put Dagon back in place. 4 But when they got up early the next day, he’d fallen face down on the ground in front of the Ark of the LORD, and his head and hands were lying cut off on the threshold. Only his trunk was left. 5 That’s why Dagon’s priests and everybody that goes in his temple still don’t step on his threshold in Ashdod.

item.title: 1 Samuel 6
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 6

The Ark Returns to Israel

1 The LORD’s Ark was in Philistine territory for seven months. 2 They called for the priests and fortunetellers, “What should we do with it? Tell us how to send it back.”

3 “Send it back with a guilt offering to Israel’s God. You’ll be healed, and you’ll find out why he keeps working against you.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 7
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 7

Samuel Judges Israel

3 Then Samuel told Israel, “If you’re coming back to the LORD with all your heart, put away the foreign gods and the images of Ashtoreth. Direct your heart to serve him only, and he’ll deliver you from the Philistines.” 4 Israel got rid of the images of Baal and Ashtoreth and served the LORD only.

item.title: 1 Samuel 8
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 8

Israel Demands a King

1 When Samuel was getting older, he made his sons judges over Israel: 2 Joel his firstborn and Abijah. They judged in Beersheba, 3 but they didn’t follow Samuel’s ways. They pursued dishonest gain, took bribes, and perverted justice.

4 Israel’s elders gathered to Samuel at Ramah, 5 “You’re getting old, and your sons don’t follow your ways. Appoint a king to govern us like the other nations. 6 That displeased Samuel. He prayed to the LORD, 7 and the LORD said,

item.title: 1 Samuel 9
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 9

Samuel told Israel, “Every one of you, go home.”

1 Sam 8:10-22

Saul Chosen as King

1 A wealthy man in Benjamin named Kish son of Abiel (son of Zeror son of Becorath son of Aphiah) 2 had a son named Saul, a handsome young man. There wasn’t a man in Israel more handsome than he was. He was head and shoulders taller than everybody.

item.title: 1 Samuel 10
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 10

Saul Anointed as King

1 Samuel took a vial of oil, poured it on Saul’s head, and kissed him,

“Hasn’t the LORD anointed you to rule his people? You’ll save Israel from their enemies round about. This will be the sign that the LORD has anointed you ruler over his heritage. 2 When you leave me today, you’ll meet two men by Rachel’s tomb in Benjamin at Zelzah. They’ll say, ‘The donkeys you went to look for they’ve found. Now your father has stopped caring about them and is worrying about you, “What am I going to do about my son?”’ 3 You’ll go on to the Oak of Tabor. Three men going up to God at Bethel will meet you, one carrying three kids, another carrying three loaves of bread, and another carrying a skin of wine. 4 They’ll greet you and give you two loaves of bread; accept them. 5 After that, you’ll come to Gibeah-elohim, where the Philistine outpost is. As you come to the town, you’ll meet a band of prophets coming down from the high place, prophesying behind a harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre. 6 The Spirit of the LORD will come on you powerfully, and you’ll prophesy with them and become another man. 7 When these signs meet you, do whatever you find to do, because God is with you. 8 Go down to Gilgal ahead of me. I’ll come to offer burnt offerings and peace offerings. Wait for a week till I come and show you what to do.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 11
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 11

Saul Defeats the Amonites

1 Nahash the Ammonite went up to lay siege to Jabesh-gilead. The men there said to him, “Make a treaty with us, and we’ll serve you.” 

2 But he said, “I’ll make one with you on this condition: I’ll gouge out your right eyes so you’ll be a disgrace in Israel.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 12
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 12

Samuel’s Farewell Address

1 Then Samuel told Israel,

“I’ve listened to you and appointed a king. 2 Now here’s the king living among you, but I’m getting old and gray; my sons are with you. I’ve lived among you since I was young. 3 Here I am. Accuse me in the presence of the LORD and his anointed. Whose ox or donkey have I taken? Who’ve I defrauded or oppressed? Who’ve I taken a bribe from to blind me? I’ll return it to you.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 13
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 13

1 Saul was 40 when he began to rule and ruled for 32 years. 2 He chose 3,000 men from Israel, 2,000 to be with him in Michmash and the hill country around Bethel and 1,000 to be with Jonathan at Gibeah in Benjamin. He sent the rest home. 3 Jonathan struck the Philistine garrison in Geba, and the Philistines heard about it. Then Saul blew the ram’s horn all over the land and said, “Hebrews, listen.” 4 All Israel heard that Saul had struck the garrison and that Israel had become repulsive to the Philistines. So Saul called the people to Gilgal.

item.title: 1 Samuel 14
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 14

Jonathan Defeats the Philistines

1 One day Jonathan said to his young armor bearer, “Let’s go over to the Philistine outpost on the other side.” He didn’t tell his father, 2 who was staying with about 600 men on the outskirts of Gibeah around the pomegranate tree in Migron. 3 Among Saul’s men was Ahijah wearing an ephod—son of Ahitub (Ichabod’s brother) son of Phinehas son of Eli the LORD’s priest at Shiloh. The people didn’t know Jonathan had gone. 4 Between the passes Jonathan was planning to use to cross over to the Philistines’ outpost, there were steep cliffs on two sides named Bozez and Seneh. 5 One went up to the north toward Michmash, the other one on the south toward Geba.                                                                                                                                                                            1 Sam 14:1-5

item.title: 1 Samuel 15
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 15

The LORD Rejects Saul

1 Then Samuel told Saul,

“The LORD of Hosts sent me to anoint you king over his people. 2 He says, ‘I’m going to settle up Amalek for opposing Israel on their way up from Egypt. 3 Strike him and destroy everything he has. Don’t spare him. Put to death man, woman, child, infant, ox, sheep, camel, and donkey.’”

item.title: 1 Samuel 16
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 16

David Anointed King

1 The LORD said to Samuel,

“How long are you going to grieve over Saul? I’ve set him aside from being king. Fill your horn with olive oil and go to Jesse of Bethlehem. I’ve selected a king from his sons.”

2 Samuel said, “How can I go there? When Saul hears about it, he’ll kill me.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 17
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 17

David and Goliath

1 The Philistines gathered their army for battle at Socoh in Judah. They camped between Socoh and Azekah in Ephes-dammim. 2 Saul and Israel came together, camped in the Valley of Elah, and drew up in battle formation to meet them. 3 The Philistines stood on the hill on one side of the valley, and Israel stood on the hill on the other side. 4 A champion from Gath named Goliath came out from the Philistine camp. He was 9ft 9 6 cubits and a span, 5 and wore a bronze helmet and scale armor that weighed 125 lbs 5,000 shekels. 6 He had bronze greaves on his shins and a bronze javelin slung between his shoulders. 7 His spear shaft was like a weaver’s beam; its spearhead weighed 15 lbs 600 shekels of iron. His shield bearer walked ahead of him. 8 He stood and shouted to the ranks of Israel,

item.title: 1 Samuel 18
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 18

Saul’s Jealousy of David

         1 When he’d finished talking to Saul, Jonathan was drawn to David and loved him as himself. 2 Saul took him that day and didn’t let him go back home. 3 Jonathan made a pact with him because he loved him as himself. 4 Jonathan took off his robe and gave it to him, along with his armor, sword, bow, and belt. 5 So David went out wherever Saul sent him and was successful. Saul set him over the fighting men. The people and Saul liked that arrangement.

item.title: 1 Samuel 19
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 19

Saul Tries to Kill David

1 Saul told Jonathan and his servants to put David to death. But Jonathan really delighted in David. 2 He told David,

“My father is trying to get you killed. Be on guard in the morning. Hide in a private place. 3 I’ll go out and stand beside him in the field where you are and talk about you. If I find out anything out, I’ll tell you.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 20
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 20

Jonathan Warns David

1 Then David fled from Naioth in Ramah and came to Jonathan, “What have I done? What am I guilty of? What sin did I commit to make your father try to kill me?”

2 He said,

“Far from it! You’re not going to die. My father doesn’t do anything big or small without letting me know. Why would he keep that from me? It’s not so.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 21
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 21

He got up and left, and Jonathan went into town.

1 Sam 20:35-42

David and the Bread of the Presence

1 Then David went to Nob to Ahimelech the priest. He came cringing to meet David, “Why are you by yourself?”

2 David told Ahimelech, “The king commissioned me with a matter, ‘Don’t let anybody know about it. I’ve directed my young men where to meet me later.’ 3 What do you have on hand? Give me five loaves of bread or whatever you can find.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 22
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 22

David at the Case of Adullam

            1 David left and got to the cave near Adullam. When his brothers and father’s family heard, they came down. 2 Everybody in distress, in debt, or disheartened gathered to him till became the captain of about 400 men.

3 He went from there to Mizpah in Moab and said to the king, “Please let my parents stay with you till I know what God will do for me.” 4 He left them with the king; they stayed with him the whole time David was in the fortress. 5 The prophet Gad told David, “Don’t stay in the fortress. Go to Judah.” So David went to the Forest of Hereth.

item.title: 1 Samuel 23
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 23

David Saves the Town of Keilah

1 People told David, “The Philistines are attacking Keilah and robbing the threshing floors.”

 2 David asked the LORD, “Should I attack these Philistines?”

“Yes, save Keilah.”

3 But David’s men said, “We’re afraid here in Judah. How much more at Keilah against the Philistines!”

item.title: 1 Samuel 24
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 24

David Spares Sual’s Life

1 When Saul came back from chasing the Philistines, he heard that David was in the Wilderness of En-gedi. 2 Saul took 3,000 chosen men from all over Israel and went to look for him and his men at the Rocks of the Wild Goats. 3 He came to the sheepfolds on the way, where there was a cave, and went in to relieve himself. David and his men were hiding in the recesses of the cave. 4 David’s men said, “This is the day the LORD told you he’d deliver your enemy to you.” David got up and cut off the edge of Saul’s robe secretly. 5 Afterward, his conscience bothered him over it. 6 He told his men, “The LORD forbid that I should reach out against his anointed.” 7 He dissuaded them with that comment and didn’t let them attack Saul. Saul got up and left the cave.

item.title: 1 Samuel 25
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 25

The Death of Samuel

1 Samuel died and Israel gathered to mourn for him. They buried him at his home in Ramah.  

David and Abigail

David went down to the Wilderness of Paran. 2aA man named Nabal in Maon had a business in Carmel. He was really rich with 3,000 sheep and 1,000 goats. (3 His wife Abigail was intelligent and beautiful, but he was harsh and rude in his dealings, a descendant of Caleb.) 2bWhile he was shearing his sheep in Carmel, 4 David heard about it in the open country. 5 He sent ten young men, and told them,

item.title: 1 Samuel 26
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 26

David Spares Saul’s Life Again

1 The people of Ziph came to Saul at Gibeah and said, “Isn’t David hiding on the Hill of Hachilah east of Jeshimon?” 2 Saul went down to the Wilderness of Ziph with 3,000 chosen men to look for him. 3 He camped on the Hill of Hachilah beside the road east of Jeshimon. David was staying in the open country. When he saw that Saul had come after him, 4 he sent out spies to make sure that Saul was coming. 5 He went to where Saul and his men had camped and saw where he lay beside Abner son of Ner, his general. The army was asleep around him.

item.title: 1 Samuel 27
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 27

David Flees to the Philistines

1 David said to himself, “Someday Saul is going to kill me. All I can do is escape to the Philistines. He’ll get tired of looking for me in Israel, and I’ll get away from him.” 2 David and his 600 men crossed over to Achish son of Maoch, king of Gath. 3 They lived with Achish, each with his family and David with his two wives Ahinoam of Jezreel and Abigail of Carmel, Nabal’s widow. 4 Saul heard that David had fled to Gath, so he quit looking for him.

item.title: 1 Samuel 28
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 28

Saul Consults the Medium at En-dor

1 At that time, the Philistines gathered their armed camps to fight Israel. Achish told David, “You can be sure that you and your men will go out with me in the camp.”

2 “All right, you’ll find out what I can do.”

“I’ll make you my bodyguard for life.”

item.title: 1 Samuel 29
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 29

The Philistines Reject David

1 The Philistines gathered their forces to Aphek while the Israelites were camping by the spring in Jezreel. 2 The Philistine lords were moving forward by hundreds and thousands; David and his men were moving forward in the rear with Achish. 3 The commanders of the Philistines said, “What are these Hebrews doing here?” Achish said, “Isn’t this David, Saul’s servant, that’s been with me these days, or years? I haven’t found a fault in him from the day he deserted to me till now.” 4 But the commanders were mad,

item.title: 1 Samuel 30
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 30

David’s Wives Are Captured

1 When David and his men got home to Ziklag on the third day, the Amalekites had made a raid on the Negev, attacked Ziklag, and burned it. 2 They took the women captive and everybody in it, big and small, but didn’t kill anybody. 3 When David and his men got there, it had been burned, and their wives, sons, and daughters had been captured. 4 They cried till they couldn’t cry anymore. 5 David’s two wives had been taken, Ahinoam and Abigail. 6 In addition, David was distressed because the people were talking about stoning him. They were bitter over losing their sons and daughters, but David took courage in the LORD his God.

item.title: 1 Samuel 31
item.description:
1 Samuel Chapter 31

The Death of Saul

1 The Philistines attacked Israel, and Israel fled and fell slain on Mount Gilboa. 2 The Philistines overtook Saul and his sons, and killed Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malci-shua. 3 The battle pressed Saul hard, and the archers seriously wounded him. 4 He told his armor bearer, “Draw your sword and run me through so these uncircumcised Philistines won’t come and run me through and torture me.” But his armor bearer was afraid to do it. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.  5 When he saw that Saul was dead, he fell on his sword too and died with him. 6 Saul died with his three sons, his armor bearer, and all his men that day. 7 When the men of Israel on the other side of the valley and beyond the Jordan, saw that Israel had fled and Saul and his sons were dead, they abandoned the towns and fled. The Philistines came and lived in them.
